data.title: "Trump accuses Bureau of Labor Statistics of manipulation, plans to dismiss official; experts warn of impact on trust in data."
data.description: According to economists, the removal of the head of the Bureau of Labor Statistics may have a negative impact on public trust in government economic data.
U.N. Security Council meets on Ukraine

A powerful mudflow surged from Russia’s Mount Elbrus, flooding Tyrnyauz, halting water and gas, and damaging homes, highways, and the town’s lifeline bridge. Locals and tourists evacuated as the region reels from this natural shock.

data.title: Darrin Anderson appointed CEO of KENDELL, a commercial door solutions provider in Minneapolis - 25 years of experience.
data.description: In a news release from Minneapolis on August 1, 2025, KENDELL, a prominent supplier of commercial door, frame, and hardware solutions, revealed the appointment of Darrin Anderson as their new Chief Executive Officer. With this development, Anderson, who boasts more than 25 years of leadership expertise, assumes the role with immediate effect.

data.title: Capitol City Brewing Company in Washington DC, shuts down after 33 years on August 28, 2025 - a landmark pub's closure.
data.description: After over 30 years of operation, Capitol City Brewing Company in Washington, D.C., will be shutting down on August 28, 2025. The establishment has been a significant part of the community, being the first brewpub to open in D.C. post-Prohibition. Known for its award-winning beers, traditional pub food, live sports screenings, and vibrant Downtown ambiance, Capitol City has been a popular spot since it first opened on August 23, 19XX.

data.title: Krystal Biotech to report EPS of $1.29 on 2025-08-04, with past performance showing shares down 12.3% in 52 weeks. Analysts discuss market sentiments on the company.
data.description: Krystal Biotech (NASDAQ: KRYS) is set to release its latest quarterly earnings report on August 4, 2025. Analysts predict an earnings per share (EPS) of $1.29. Last quarter, the company missed EPS by $0.32, leading to a 1.91% drop in share price the next day. The stock was trading at $153.87 as of July 31, down 12.3% over the last 52 weeks. Market sentiment and analyst opinions play a crucial role in influencing stock performance. Investors will be looking for positive guidance from the company for the next quarter to drive stock price growth.
Week Ahead: Economic Indicators 4th – 8th August (US)

data.title: Tidewater to release Q2 earnings report soon with expected EPS at $0.41. Past EPS and share price changes are noted. Analysts predict a potential 39.97% upside with an Outperform rating. Shares trading at $50.01, down 43.41% in last 52 weeks.
data.description: Tidewater (NYSE: TDW) will release its quarterly earnings report on 2025-08-04, with analysts expecting an EPS of $0.41. The previous earnings beat EPS by $0.24, leading to a 5.84% share price increase. Tidewater's stock was trading at $50.01 on July 31, down 43.41% over the past 52 weeks. Analysts rate the company as Outperform, with a one-year price target of $70.00, representing a potential 39.97% upside. Understanding market sentiments and guidance will be critical for investors ahead of the announcement.

data.title: PCS Edventures! buys back 3,736,170 shares, enhancing its position as a provider of STEM education programs.
data.description: On August 1, 2025, in Meridian, Idaho, PCS Edventures!, Inc. disclosed that they have bought back 3,736,170 of their shares from the market between July 7, 2025, and July 28, 2025. PCS Edventures is a prominent provider of STEM education programs for students in TK-12 grades.

data.title: Bitcoin marks 8th anniversary of Block Size Wars with significant value growth post-independence from miner control on Aug. 1, 2017.
data.description: Since August 1, 2017, the value of Bitcoin has significantly increased following the Bitcoin community's move to break free from miner control.

data.title: Canaan Inc. adopts Bitcoin (BTC) as primary reserve asset in new policy, focusing on growth and liquidity.
data.description: Canaan Inc. recently announced its new cryptocurrency holding policy, highlighting Bitcoin as the primary reserve asset to meet strategic growth and liquidity requirements. This move comes as part of the company's strategy to secure its financial position in the volatile crypto market. Canaan Inc. aims to maintain a substantial amount of Bitcoin in its reserves to support its long-term objectives. The decision signifies a shift towards a more Bitcoin-centric approach within the company. This policy update reflects Canaan Inc.'s commitment to adapting to the evolving crypto landscape by ensuring a significant portion of its assets – a noteworthy development in the industry.

data.title: New tool Gemini considers + executes crypto trades. Examined for effectiveness, it aids in some aspects but not all.
data.description: Gemini, a trading platform, appears sophisticated, but its functionality for actual trading was tested. Sample trades were conducted to assess its performance. The review delves into the platform's strengths and weaknesses, highlighting areas where it excels and where it falls short.

data.title: NextNav and Oscilloquartz team up to improve 5G timing solutions for critical infrastructure, aiming to provide alternative to GPS for systems such as data centers, utilities and emergency services.
data.description: NextNav, a top player in resilient Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) technologies, revealed a significant partnership with Oscilloquartz, a prominent global leader in time synchronization solutions. This collaboration aims to highlight the potential of 5G-powered terrestrial PNT and Oscilloquartz’s established timing technology. Together, they plan to offer a reliable complement and backup to GPS, ensuring the continuity of essential systems such as data centers, utilities, and emergency services.

data.title: Fed doesn't cut rates despite Trump pressure, reveals internal divide. Interest rates remain at 4.25%-4.50%. Two Governors dissent for first time since 1993.
data.description: Amid mounting expectations for a pivotal Federal Reserve meeting, the central bank's decision to maintain interest rates at 4.25%-4.50% in July did not come as a surprise to the market. However, the meeting revealed a significant internal discord within the Federal Open Market Committee. This marked the first time since 1993 that two permanent members of the Fed's Board of Governors, Michelle Bowman and Christopher Waller, openly dissented, advocating for an immediate 25 basis-point rate reduction. This level of dissent among members has not been witnessed since the era of Alan Greenspan. The development highlights a rare split within the Fed, shedding light on the notable internal tensions. More information is available on Benzinga.com.
